name: Universal red-cell donor blood type
question_text: Which ABO/Rh blood group is considered the universal donor for packed red blood cells?
general_feedback: Type O negative red cells carry neither A nor B surface antigens nor the RhD antigen, so they do not trigger ABO or RhD incompatibility reactions in any recipient.
correct_feedback: Correct — No A, B, or RhD antigens on the red cells — compatible for transfusion into any recipient.

answers.feedback: No A, B, or RhD antigens on the red cells — compatible for transfusion into any recipient.
AB+ is the universal plasma recipient, not a universal red-cell donor.
O+ is compatible with Rh-positive recipients but not with Rh-negative ones.
AB negative red cells carry both A and B antigens and are not universally compatible.
